channel.title: Blogs on Michael Healy's ePortfolio
channel.description: Recent content in Blogs on Michael Healy's ePortfolio

item.title: Curriculum Vitae
item.description: Education PhD English, Expected August 2019 Florida State University, Tallahassee, Florida Specialization Rhetoric and Composition MA, English August 2016 University of Nebraska at Omaha, Omaha, Nebraska Comprehensive Exam: Rhetoric and Composition, November 2015 Graduate Certificate, Advanced Writing (15 Hours): Theory, craft, pedagogy, and publication of Creative Nonfiction using video, digital texts, experimental genres, nature writing, and archival research. Relevant Coursework College Writing Instruction (Seminar) Field Based Research Methods (Seminar)

item.title: Administrative Philosophy
item.description: In examining my administrative philosophy, I see four major threads emerging. First, that writing majors need to leverage and be adaptable to local contexts. Second, that they should be built on inquiry into the discipline of Rhetoric and Composition. Third, that they build on local expertise and needs. And fourth, that an effective writing major is built on praxis. This builds upon the idea that effective writing curriculum and pedagogy is responsive to and built out of local contexts, be responsive to local needs, and draw upon the interests and expertise of faculty, staff, and students within the university.

item.title: Teaching Philosophy
item.description: Old - To Update I view writing as the extension of thought into a physical form. Writing is not only able to convey information it also creates and changes meaning. Writing is in dialogue with the writer, the audience, and the broader context of culture and society. I want students to be able to enter into this dialogue as full participants, not just people who just consume and regurgitate the thoughts of others.

item.title: ENC 3021 - Rhetoric
item.description: Selection of feedback and materials from my teaching of ENC 3021: Rhetoric. Includes course sites, evaluation materials, lessons examples, and course materials.

item.title: You Are Here 2018
item.description: Presented “Invention, Place, and Play: Exploring a Networked and Embodied Composition Pedagogy” at You Are Here: An Interdisciplinary Conference on Place, Space, and Embodiment

item.title: ENC 1101 - Freshman Composition and Rhetoric
item.description: Materials and feedback from my teaching of ENC 1101. Includes course sites, evaluation materials, lessons examples, and course materials.

item.title: College Composition Committee
item.description: Have been a member of the college composition committee at FSU since the fall of 2016. Have worked on developing materials for GTAs, rubrics, syllabi, lesson plans, and in-class activities. Have revised resource strands for teachers and instructors. Have worked to select a new textbook for ENC 1101 and developed teaching materials for the book. Well help facilitate workshops for on going teacher development in the spring of 2019.

item.title: Johnston Digital Studio Coordinator 2018-2019
item.description: Information about and work with the digital studio will go here: Consultant Coordinator

item.title: College Composition @ FSU
item.description: Have taught within the college composition program at UNO and FSU. Taught English Composition I and Composition II at UNO. Have taught ENC 1101 and ENC 2135 at FSU. My pedagogy focuses on exploration, observation, play, and collaboration inside and outside the class.

item.title: Publications
item.description: Publications and works in progress will go here.

item.title: ENC 2135 - Genres, Research, and Context
item.description: Materials and feedback from my teaching of ENC 2135. Includes course sites, evaluation materials, lessons examples, and course materials.

item.title: SAMLA 2017
item.description: Presented “The Efficacy of Play as an Organizational and Pedagogical Theory”

item.title: Feminisms and Rhetorics 2017
item.description: Presented “Circulation, Narrative, and Human Rights: New Materialism meets Spectacular Rhetoric” on the panel “Not Stepping on Anyone’s Toes: A Beginner’s Guide to Conscientious Conversation for Human Rights Activism.”

item.title: CCCC 2017
item.description: Presented “Ludic Revision: Potentials, Play, and Choice” on the panel “A Symbiotic (and Pedagogical) Relationship: Forging an Alliance between Creative Nonfiction and Composition.”

item.title: Cccc 2016
item.description: Presented a work in progress at the Qualitative Research Network.

item.title: Feminisms and Rhetorics 2015
item.description: Presented “Celebrating Grandma: (Re)Making (or Appropriating?) Agency in the Archive” on the panel “(Re)Making Story: Agency and Gender in Teaching, Researching, and Writing.”

item.title: Cccc 2015
item.description: Presented a Work In Progress at the Research Network Forum.

item.title: Writing Center University of Nebraska Omaha
